overview: A thin orchestration layer designed to provide coding agents (like Cursor) with long-term memory, architectural context, and modular ownership. It implements a write-back loop for continuous learning, a multi-agent coordination system via internal messaging, and an iterative execution engine (RALPH) to ensure code quality through testing.

requirements.title: Ownership Registry & Management
requirements.description: Establish a system to map specific code modules and directories to dedicated Agent Teams/Roles to preserve architectural intent and specialized knowledge.
success_criteria: Registry resolves file ownership using longest-prefix-match logic.
The system prevents ownership conflicts (one primary owner per file).
CLI supports 'ace own', 'ace who', and 'ace list-owners' with sub-second response times.

sub_requirements.title: Agent Registry (agents.yaml)
sub_requirements.description: Centralized YAML store for agent metadata (ID, name, role, email, memory file).
success_criteria: Manual and autonomous agent creation is logged correctly in .ace/agents.yaml.

sub_requirements.title: Module Mapping (ownership.yaml)
sub_requirements.description: Mapping of file paths to agent IDs.
success_criteria: Changes to ownership are reflected immediately in the registry and persist across CLI restarts.

requirements.title: Dynamic Context Builder
requirements.description: A component that composes a context-slice (Memory Slice) for agent calls based on target files, roles, and task types.
success_criteria: Injected context includes global rules, agent playbook, recent ADRs, and session history.
The system generates different prompt-prefixes for 'implement', 'review', 'debug', 'refactor', and 'plan' tasks.

sub_requirements.title: Task Type Framing
sub_requirements.description: Specific instructional wrappers for different development activities.
success_criteria: Agent output format varies predictably based on the task type framing (e.g., debug mode focuses on [mis-XXX] extraction).

requirements.title: Write-back Learning Pipeline
requirements.description: A mechanism to extract lessons, strategies, and pitfalls from agent outputs and update the agent's specific .mdc playbook.
success_criteria: The orchestrator correctly identifies and parses [str-XXX], [mis-XXX], and [dec-XXX] tags from LLM responses.
Delta updates to .mdc files preserve existing file structure and frontmatter.
Helpful/Harmful counters are incremented/decremented based on task success/failure.

sub_requirements.title: Reflection Engine
sub_requirements.description: Secondary LLM pass to analyze task results and generate structured JSON updates.
success_criteria: Reflection accurately identifies why a strategy failed by correlating it with test results.

requirements.title: Execution & RALPH Loop Motor
requirements.description: The engine responsible for running the agent, capturing output, and iterating until tests pass.
success_criteria: CLI can execute cursor-agent in a headless mode and capture JSON-streamed output.
The RALPH loop successfully executes the sequence: Reason -> Action -> Learn -> Progress -> Halt.
Execution terminates automatically when user-defined tests pass or max iterations are reached.

sub_requirements.title: Session Logging
sub_requirements.description: Persistent storage of every interaction in .ace/sessions/ for audit and continuity.
success_criteria: Sessions are saved as Git-trackable Markdown files with timestamps.

requirements.title: Multi-Agent Coordination & Communication
requirements.description: System for agents to interact, debate architectural changes, and manage dependencies via formal messaging.
success_criteria: Agent Mail (inbox/sent) persists as Markdown files in .ace/mail/.
The system can detect conflicts when a task spans multiple agent ownership boundaries.

sub_requirements.title: Consensus Protocol
sub_requirements.description: A debate mechanism where agents propose changes and a referee resolves conflicts.
success_criteria: The system escalates to a human user if consensus isn't reached within 3 rounds.

sub_requirements.title: SOP Implementation
sub_requirements.description: Standard Operating Procedures for onboarding, reviews, and audits.
success_criteria: Onboarding generates a status summary for a subsystem that is usable by a new agent.

requirements.title: Token Consumption Control
requirements.description: Configurable modes to control token usage and operational costs.
success_criteria: Low mode disables multi-agent debate and minimizes context injection.
High mode enables proactive refactoring suggestions and deep context analysis.
CLI command 'ace config tokens --mode' updates the behavior globally.

requirements.title: UI/UX Integration (Google Stitch)
requirements.description: Integration with Google Stitch for AI-driven design mockups and code extraction.
success_criteria: UI agents can generate 'Vibe Design' prompts suitable for Stitch.
CLI supports syncing code (Tailwind/Flutter) from a Stitch canvas URL into the codebase.

requirements.title: Global Memory & Standards (AGENTS.md)
requirements.description: Maintenance of a project-root AGENTS.md file as a cross-tool communication hub.
success_criteria: AGENTS.md contains an up-to-date table of agent roles and their .mdc file mappings.
Global standards and recent ADRs are automatically reflected in the AGENTS.md file.
